Um, water reform is, is going to help, um, you know,
for those, particularly those councils who look to join up
and deliver their water services differently, I think
that will make a big difference.
Um, and I think, you know,
better allowing councils more flexibility
and better use, uh, with PPPs, um, all those things could,
could absolutely assist in, in reducing costs as well.
Um, and I think tho those would also be on the table.
So yeah, I did focus a little bit on revenue there,
but there are things, um, which we're also talking
with the Prime Minister and others on of, of,
of some wins we can help, you know, relieve
that burden on, on councils.
Alright, well look, Mr.
Peacock, I hope that, um,
broadening the discussion out a little.
I mean, I know, um,
and every one of us as a, as a, an electorate MP
is hearing from people like yourself of the burden of rates.
So you're not a lone voice.
And it's something that we are all very conscious of, of as,
um, our, each of our constituents, um,
starts to feel this precious.
So thank you for bringing it to us.
Um, and it's been interesting

certain explanation
and possibly some, some strategies for the future.
So thank you very much. Um,
or we time this afternoon, thank.
